summary: Fixed three startup-hang bugs: (1) MCP server connections now wrapped in tokio::time::timeout(15s) — if a server like 'npx @playwright/mcp@latest' takes too long or never responds, it is skipped with a warn! log and the rest of startup continues; (2) Plugin initialize() calls now wrapped in tokio::time::timeout(10s) — hung plugins are killed, marked PluginStatus::Failed, and skipped; (3) Web server (ava serve) now binds the TCP listener and prints the 'listening on' message BEFORE calling WebState::init, so the port is claimed immediately — MCP and plugin init are still bounded by the timeouts from fixes 1 and 2, so total worst-case blocking is num_servers*15s + num_plugins*10s rather than unbounded.

notes.web_server_change: TcpListener::bind now called before WebState::init in run_server(); port is claimed before any MCP/plugin I/O begins
notes.timeout_log_level: warn! for both timeout and error cases — never fails silently
notes.plugin_cleanup: On timeout, process.shutdown() is called before marking Failed to avoid zombie processes
